entry.title: Reset a Git Remote Repository
entry.content:
Reset a Git Remote Repository

Needs:

Delete the git remote repository, including all files in the remote repository, all commit records, and make it a brand new empty repository.

Scenes:

  1. To rebuild the original project, you need to clear the original history or all code, but do not create a new remote repository.

  2. A submitted file contains sensitive information (password files, etc.).

  3. Need to delete the remote repository for some reason.

1 Create a separate branch

2 Delete local files

If you are going to empty the remote repository, delete all local files (the files are still useful and can be backed up locally); if you delete some files or ignore sensitive files (such as passwords and other information), you can add ignore rules to the .gitignore file.

delete all local files

3 Re-add local files

4 Commit the current branch

5 Delete the original master branch

If there are other branches, delete them as well

6 Change the current branch to the main branch

7 The remote warehouse that will force the current branch to be pushed

Then all the previous records of the remote repository will no longer exist, only the current latest submission record.

entry.title: Build a personal blog with Github Pages and Hexo
entry.content:
Build a personal blog with Github Pages and Hexo

The first blog will briefly record the construction process. In fact, there are many online tutorials. I just take a little time to record the process. There are not many pits in the construction. System is MacOS.

Why choose Github Pages and Hexo?

I suddenly wanted to build a blog and play around with front-end development. I use Github Pages mainly because it is free and I don't have to spend money to rent a server.

In Jekyll and Hexo, the two static webpage generators, Jekyll relies on Ruby, the speed of static site generation will be slower and slower as the site content increases, while Hexo relies on node, the main focus of speed, there are a lot of available free open source themes and a very active Chinese community.

Init Github Pages

  1. Register a Github account, note down Username and email address
  2. Follow the official tutorial create a new Repositories, note that the repository must be named as<Username.github.io>.

Init Git

Xcode on mac contains git. View version git --version. MacOS also comes with SSH

Git Configuration

Open terminal, setup user.name and user.email

SSH Password-free login

Generate SSH public and private keys:

where -C means comment string, like email address; -f specify key file storage file name

Distribute the generated public key to the server open ~/id_rsa.pub and copy to you Github web setting -> SSH

Test SSH

You would see:

Choose yes

Install Hexo

Hexo need node environment, if you haven't install brew on your computer

Then install Node through brew, see Node Document

Use the node package management tool npm to install hexo, with its own source very very slow, there are two ways to use domestic source installation.

Temporary use of domestic sources:

Set the global source configuration (equivalent to creating a .npmrc file) to use domestic sources:

After installation, check the currently installed modules (packages), note that Hexo is globally installed, add parameters -g

Plug-in

It's best to make a series of plugin changes before starting work, perfecting support for latex, git and image insertion

Install and use the theme Next

Note that the original Next github page is no longer maintained!

Must be hexo-theme-next

Install Next

Upgrade can be executed

\(\LaTeX\) Support:

We have installed hexo-renderer-kramed and hexo-math. Then modify _config.yml

per_page: false : Turn off the rendering formula of each page, the access speed of the page without the formula will not be affected, but each file needs to be manually add mathjax: true in the markdown file header

Inline formula: \(\Im, \imath, \jmath, \Bbbk, \ell, \mho, \wp, \Re, \circledS\)

Interline formula:

\[ dy/dx, \mathrm{d}y/\mathrm{d}x, \frac{dy}{dx}, \frac{\mathrm{d}y}{\mathrm{d}x}, \frac{\partial^2}{\partial x_1\partial x_2}y \]

Multi-line formula:

\[ \begin{align} a &= b + c \label{eq3} \\ x &= yz \label{eq4}\\ l &= m - n \label{eq5} \end{align} \]

Write blog with Hexo

Init blog folder

Hexo will form the following working directory in the specified folder

where

node_modules: Dependency packages public：Store the generated pages scaffolds：Some templates source：store your articles themes：Next theme _config.yml: Blog Profile

You can configure most of the parameters here.

New blog

Create New blog

This will create md file and resource folder with the same name as "new document title" use template hexofolder/scaffolds/post if you set (_config.yml: post_asset_folder: true).

Writing and publish

entry.title: Variational Inference
entry.content:
The goal of variational inference is to approximate the conditional probability of a latent variable \(x\) under the observed variable \(y\) (in the context of dimensionality reduction, our data). We choose a tractable distribution \(q(x; v)\), where \(v\) represents model hyperparameters, as an approximation to simulate the intractable true distribution \(p(x \mid y)\).

We can rewrite \(\log p(y)\) to decompose it into two terms. can be written as the sum of the following two terms:

\[ \begin{equation} \log p(y)=\underbrace{\int q(x) \log p(y, x) dx -\int q(x) \log q(x) d x}_{L(q(x)), E L O B(\text { Evidence Lower Bound })}+\underbrace{\int q(x) \log \frac{q(x)}{p(x \mid y)} d x}_{K L(q(x) \| p(x \mid y))}. \label{eq1} \end{equation} \]

The latter term is known as the Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence, which is a method of quantifying the difference between two distributions, and is either larger than or equal to zero at all times. It equals 0 if and only if the two distributions are identical .

Since \(\log p(y)\) is a fixed quantity for a given set of observations, maximizing the is equivalent to minimizing the KL divergence, i.e., minimizing the distance between the two distributions.

This conclusion can also be obtained by use Jensen’s inequality to show that

\[ \begin{align} \log p(y) \geq ELOB. \label{eq2} \end{align} \]

Based on this fact, instead of trying to maximize the likelihood directly, we can try to maximize the lower bound of the likelihood and still can optimize the parameters as well as make predictions.

Proof of Equation \(\eqref{eq1}\), approach showing the relationship between marginal likelihood, ELOB, and KL divergence:

\[ \begin{equation} \begin{aligned} \log p(y) &=\log p(y, x)-\log p(x \mid y) \\ &=\log \frac{p(y, x)}{q(x)}-\log \frac{p(x \mid y)}{q(x)} \\ &=\log p(y, x)-\log q(x)-\log \frac{p(x \mid y)}{q(x)} \\ &=\log p(y, x)-\log q(x)+\log \frac{q(x)}{p(x \mid y)} \end{aligned} \end{equation} \]

Integrate \(q(x)\) on both sides of the above equation

\[ \begin{equation} \begin{aligned} \int q(x) \log p(y) d x &=\int q(x) \log p(y, x) d x-\int q(x) \log q(x) d x \\ &+\int q(x) \log \frac{q(x)}{p(x \mid y)} d x \end{aligned} \end{equation} \]

Since \(q(x)\) and \(p(y)\) are independent, \(\int q(x) \log p(y) dx = \log p(y)\), then

\[ \begin{align} \log p(y)=\underbrace{\int q(x) \log p(y, x) dx -\int q(x) \log q(x) d x}_{L(q(x)), E L O B(\text { Evidence Lower Bound })}+\underbrace{\int q(x) \log \frac{q(x)}{p(x \mid y)} d x}_{K L(q(x) \| p(x \mid y))}. \end{align} \]

Another approach using Jensen’s inequality (Equation \(\eqref{eq2}\)):

\[ \begin{equation} \begin{aligned} \log P(y) &=\log \left(\int P(y, x) d x\right) \\ &=\log \left(\int\left(\frac{P(y, x)}{q(x)} q(x)\right) d x\right) \\ &=\log E_{x q(x)}\left[\frac{P(y, x)}{q(x)}\right] \\ & \geq E_{x q(x)} \log \left(\frac{P(y, x)}{q(x)}\right) \\ &=E_{x q(x)} \log P(y, x)-E_{x q(x)} \log q(x) \\ &=\int q(x) \log P(y, x) d x-\int q(x) \log q(x) d x \end{aligned} \end{equation} \]
